Manifesto

WE DO NOT CONFORM.
WE ENGINEER.

RAWx bridges raw industrial aesthetics with absolute manufacturing precision. We operate at the intersection of apparel and systems engineering — where every seam is a specification, every GSM is non-negotiable, and every colorway is a deliberate signal.

Designed for the relentless. Built for global scale.

Non-Conformance

We reject conventional supply chain theatrics. No broker margins, no quality dilution. Direct manufacturing oversight from Bangladesh's industrial core.

Predictability as Design

Every order executes against a fixed spec sheet. Tolerances are published, defect rates are tracked, and accountability is structural — not optional.

Aesthetic as Signal

Acid colors. Brutal type. Industrial silhouettes. RAWx communicates competence through form before a single thread is examined.

// QA Protocol — Node 1-2-3 Physical Intervention
Defect Rate: <0.8%
Infrastructure

NODE 1-2-3
PHYSICAL
QA INTERCEPT

We intercept production at three critical junctures — before, during, and after assembly.

Node 1 fires at cutting stage, catching fabric deviation and marker errors before a single seam is stitched. Node 2 monitors stitching tolerances and seam integrity in-line. Node 3 executes final hardware assembly inspection and packing verification.

This is not a quality checklist. It is a physical QA intervention architecture.

Cutting Accuracy 99.2% Node 1 — Fabric Stage
Stitch Tolerance ±1.2mm Node 2 — In-line Monitor
Defect Interception <0.8% Node 3 — Final Assembly
On-Time Ship Rate 97.4% 30-Day Production Cycle
1 // Node 01 — Pre-Stitch Cutting Floor Intercept
  • Fabric shrinkage pre-wash testing
  • Marker lay efficiency audit (≥87%)
  • Colour lot verification against master swatch
  • GSM deviation check vs specification
  • Cut bundle tagging and traceability log
2 // Node 02 — In-Line Stitching Monitor
  • Seam allowance ±1.5mm tolerance gate
  • SPI (stitches per inch) live audit
  • Thread tension and skip-stitch detection
  • Alignment check at collar/cuff join points
  • In-line defect pull-out and reorder protocol
3 // Node 03 — Final Gate Assembly & Pack Verification
  • Hardware / trims torque and attachment test
  • Measurement spec recheck vs buyer PO
  • AQL 2.5 sampling on finished garments
  • Packing integrity and labelling compliance
  • Digital inspection report issued per shipment
